prompt: Add cobra for CLI commands with help and version

turns.content:
<task>
Replace the basic flag parsing with cobra for proper CLI subcommands, help, and version.
</task>

1	 package main
2	 
3	 import (
4	 	"flag"
5	 	"fmt"
6	 )
7	 
8	 var version = "dev"
9	 
10	 func main() {
11	 	v := flag.Bool("version", false, "print version")
12	 	flag.Parse()
13	 	if *v {
14	 		fmt.Println(version)
15	 		return
16	 	}
17	 	fmt.Println("Hello")
18	 }

arguments.query: cobra Go CLI framework rootCmd version subcommand example

turns.content:
1. Cobra User Guide - https://github.com/spf13/cobra/blob/main/site/content/user_guide.md
   go get github.com/spf13/cobra. rootCmd with RunE. AddCommand for subcommands.
2. Cobra Generator - cobra-cli init.

arguments.title: Cobra CLI framework Go
arguments.content:
go get github.com/spf13/cobra
Root command: &cobra.Command{Use: 'app', Short: 'desc', RunE: func}
Subcommand: rootCmd.AddCommand(subCmd)
Version: rootCmd.Version = version (auto-adds --version flag)
Persistent flags: rootCmd.PersistentFlags().StringVarP(&var, 'name', 'n', default, 'help')
Local flags: cmd.Flags().BoolVarP(...)

turns.content: Finding recorded: Cobra CLI framework Go

arguments.command: cd /project && go mod tidy 2>&1 && go build . 2>&1

arguments.message: Cobra is set up. The CLI now has auto-generated help, --version flag, and a `run` subcommand. Add more subcommands with `rootCmd.AddCommand()`. The version flag uses the `version` variable which you can set at build time with `-ldflags`.

arguments.summary: Replaced flag parsing with cobra. Added root command with version, run subcommand, auto-generated help. Builds and runs.
arguments.final_answer: Cobra CLI framework set up with root command, --version flag, and `run` subcommand. Auto-generated help and shell completion. Add subcommands with rootCmd.AddCommand().
